process_overview: The Academic Program Evaluation & Launch process governs how Crescent University of Management & Sciences evaluates the performance of its existing programs and manages two possible outcomes: launching a new academic program when performance is satisfactory and demand warrants expansion, or closing intake for underperforming programs. It is the primary process executed under the Academic Affairs grandparent function. The process begins with a gateway decision on whether program performance is satisfactory. The Yes path triggers a structured seven-step new program development and approval workflow, incorporating two critical conditional gateways — an accommodations feasibility check and HEC regulatory approval — either of which can terminate the process early if conditions are not met. The No path routes directly to intake closure. Both paths converge at the end event. The process involves five roles across four functions. The Curriculum Coordinator and Program Proposal Officer handle the design phase; the Dean of Academics manages HQ presentation and program launch; the Campus Facilities Manager assesses infrastructure readiness and executes closures; and the HEC Liaison Officer manages regulatory approval. Key objectives include achieving HEC NOC approval within 90 days of submission, ensuring zero program launches without confirmed accommodation capacity, and completing intake closure administrative actions within 5 working days of the performance decision.

process_task.value_rationale: AI: 40.0% VA / 40.0% BVA / 20.0% NVA. Automate curriculum document submission to reduce NVA.

task.task_overview: Design the complete academic curriculum for the proposed new program, covering all semesters, module titles, credit hours, pre-requisites, learning outcomes, assessment strategies, and faculty qualification requirements. The Curriculum Coordinator references HEC minimum criteria for the discipline, benchmarks against comparable accredited programs, and consults the industry advisory board to ensure relevance. Output is a full curriculum document submitted to the Program Proposal Officer for inclusion in the formal proposal.

job.description: <font color="#000000">The Dean of Academics is the senior authority on all academic matters within the university. They provide strategic direction for program development, quality assurance, and academic policy. The Dean reviews and approves curriculum proposals, leads presentations to University Headquarters for executive sign-off, issues program launch directives, and authorises first intake enrollment windows. They assign faculty leads, oversee regulatory approvals, and take final accountability across the entire academic program lifecycle — from initial proposal through to launch or closure. The role demands strong leadership, stakeholder management, and the ability to align academic vision with institutional and regulatory expectations.</font>
job.name: Dean of Academics

job.description: Responsible for developing and reviewing academic curricula aligned with HEC standards and industry requirements. The officer designs course structures, learning outcomes, credit frameworks, and assessment strategies across all semesters. In parallel, they prepare formal proposal documents justifying new program introductions, including market demand analysis, competitor benchmarking, enrollment projections, and resource cost estimates. They collaborate with the Dean of Academics, industry advisors, and regulatory bodies to ensure all academic content meets accreditation requirements. Strong analytical and documentation skills are essential. The officer serves as the primary link between academic design and institutional approval processes.
job.name: Curriculum & Proposal Officer

job.description: Oversees two critical functions: ensuring the university operates within applicable laws and regulations, and managing the operational readiness of all physical facilities. In the context of program development, the officer assesses classroom, laboratory, and residential accommodation capacity against projected enrollment numbers, producing formal feasibility reports. They also act as HEC Liaison, compiling and submitting No Objection Certificate applications including curriculum documents, faculty profiles, and financial sustainability statements. During program closures, they coordinate decommissioning of timetables, classroom reservations, and lab bookings. The role requires meticulous attention to regulatory detail and strong coordination with multiple internal departments.
job.name: Compliance & Facilities Officer

job.description: A senior professional from a relevant industry who provides external perspective on curriculum relevance and labour market alignment. Formally consulted during curriculum design to ensure new academic programs produce graduates meeting current employer expectations. The advisor benchmarks proposed curricula against real-world industry practices, identifies emerging skill gaps, and recommends content adjustments. Their input strengthens HEC approval applications and improves graduate employability outcomes. The role is advisory and consultative with no operational responsibilities, focused entirely on ensuring academic programs remain practically relevant, forward-looking, and aligned with the evolving demands of the industry the program serves.
job.name: Industry Advisor

process_task.value_rationale: AI: 0.0% VA / 80.0% BVA / 20.0% NVA. Automate proposal submission to reduce NVA.
process_task.bva_business_goal: Automate proposal submission to reduce NVA.

task.task_overview: Compile the formal program need proposal document that provides the evidence base justifying introduction of the new program. The Program Proposal Officer conducts market demand analysis, reviews labor market data and competitor offerings, produces enrollment projections and resource cost estimates, and integrates the curriculum document into a structured HEC-format proposal pack. The completed proposal is submitted to the Dean of Academics for review before HQ presentation.

task.task_overview: Present the completed program need proposal and curriculum to University Headquarters leadership for executive review and approval. The Dean of Academics leads the presentation, articulating the strategic rationale, market opportunity, resource requirements, and financial projections. Headquarters may request revisions or additional information before granting approval to proceed to the accommodations feasibility check. Approval from HQ is a mandatory prerequisite before any further steps are taken.

job.description: The Vice Chancellor is the highest academic and administrative authority of the university, responsible for approving major institutional decisions including new academic program launches. They evaluate proposals presented by the Dean of Academics, assessing strategic rationale, market opportunity, financial projections, and resource requirements. Their approval is a mandatory prerequisite before program development can proceed. The Vice Chancellor ensures all initiatives align with the university's long-term strategic goals and financial sustainability, exercising ultimate accountability for decisions affecting the academic portfolio, infrastructure investment, and the university's competitive positioning in the higher education sector.
job.name: Vice Chancellor

task.task_overview: Assess and report on the current availability of classrooms, laboratories, and student residential accommodation needed to support the projected intake of the proposed new program. The Campus Facilities Manager reviews the space allocation register, checks residential capacity against projected enrollment numbers, and produces a formal Accommodations Feasibility Report. This report feeds directly into the gateway decision: if sufficient capacity exists the process proceeds; if not, the process is terminated at this stage.

task.task_overview: Prepare and submit the formal No Objection Certificate (NOC) application to the Higher Education Commission of Pakistan for the proposed new program. The HEC Liaison Officer compiles the complete regulatory application package including curriculum documents, faculty profile registers, facilities declarations, and financial sustainability statements, submits to the HEC online portal, tracks application status, responds to any HEC information requests, and communicates the final approval or rejection outcome to the Dean of Academics.

task.task_overview: Formally launch the new academic program following HEC NOC approval. The Dean of Academics issues the program launch directive, assigns the program lead faculty member, activates the program in the student information system, authorises the first-intake enrollment window, and coordinates with the admissions team on marketing and student recruitment. The launch is communicated internally via an official academic notice and externally through the university website and HEC program registry update.

job.description: Responsible for attracting, evaluating, and enrolling students into university programs. When a new academic program is launched, the Admissions Officer coordinates with the Dean of Academics to execute marketing campaigns, manage student inquiries, process applications, and fill the first-intake enrollment window. They maintain recruitment pipelines, organise open days, and manage communications targeting prospective students. The officer ensures all enrollment processes comply with university admission criteria and regulatory requirements. Their timely execution of recruitment activities is critical to achieving target intake numbers for newly launched programs and sustaining overall institutional enrollment health across all academic offerings.
job.name: Admissions Officer

process_task.value_rationale: AI: 0.0% VA / 75.0% BVA / 25.0% NVA. Automate notifications to reduce NVA.

task.task_overview: Execute the administrative closure of underperforming program intakes where the program performance evaluation has determined that continuation is not viable. The Campus Facilities Manager coordinates the decommissioning of timetable allocations, classroom reservations, and laboratory bookings associated with the closed intake. Notifies the Registrar, Finance, and Student Services departments to update enrollment records, freeze new admissions, and arrange transitional support for any currently enrolled students affected by the closure.

job.name: Compliance & Facilities Officer

job.description: Maintains all official student academic records and oversees the administrative integrity of enrollment data. When a program intake is closed, the Registrar updates enrollment records, freezes new admissions for the affected program, and ensures historical academic data is accurately preserved. Coordinates record transfers, manages transcript requests, and maintains compliance with data retention policies. Supports new program launches by setting up program records in the student information system. Their role is central to ensuring accurate, reliable, and legally compliant documentation is maintained throughout the full academic program lifecycle from initial enrollment through to program closure.
job.name: Registrar

job.description: Manages financial operations, budgeting, and resource allocation related to academic programs. During new program development, the Finance Manager provides cost estimates and financial sustainability projections incorporated into HEC proposal packages. When a program intake is closed, they freeze billing, halt new admission fee processing, and reconcile outstanding financial obligations for affected students. Supports program launches by authorising budget allocation for faculty appointments and marketing. Ensures all program-related financial activities comply with internal controls and audit requirements, while maintaining the university's long-term financial sustainability and accurate reporting across all active and closing academic programs.
job.name: Finance Manager

job.description: Provides welfare, guidance, and transitional support to students throughout their academic journey. When a program intake is closed, the Student Services Coordinator identifies all affected enrolled students and arranges transitional support including academic counselling, program transfer guidance, and personal welfare assistance. Acts as the primary contact for affected students, managing sensitive communications and ensuring every student has a clear pathway forward. During new program launches, supports onboarding of first-intake students. Their role is essential in maintaining student wellbeing and institutional trust during periods of significant academic change, ensuring no student is disadvantaged by institutional decisions.
job.name: Student Services Coordinator
